commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(inference): eliminate per-token/per-head allocations in dense forward (#3305)

## What changed
- Added \ms_norm_into()\ that writes to a pre-allocated output buffer
- \DenseTransformerBlock::forward()\: single \
ormed\ buffer allocated once and reused for both attention-norm and
FFN-norm passes (previously 2seq_len separate Vec allocations per block)
- \DenseModel::forward()\: uses \ms_norm_into()\ for final norm
- \DenseAttention::forward()\: hoists \scores\ and \weights\ vectors out
of the inner (head position) loop into a single pair of pre-allocated
buffers (previously O(num_heads seq_len) allocations)

## Why
The dense forward pass allocated temporary vectors inside tight loops:
- RMSNorm: \ ms_norm()\ returned a new \Vec<f32>\ per token per layer,
then \xtend()\d into the normed buffer O(layers seq_len) allocations
- Attention: \scores\ and \weights\ vectors allocated per head per
position O(num_heads seq_len) allocations

For a 32-head, 24-layer model with 512-token context: ~24K norm allocs +
~8M attention allocs eliminated.

## What was tested
- All 19 dense_forward tests pass (including new \
est_rms_norm_into_matches_rms_norm\)
- \cargo test -p bitnet-inference --lib dense_forward
--no-default-features --features cpu\
- No behavioral change (pure refactor: same outputs, fewer allocations)

## Risks
None \ ms_norm()\ now delegates to \ ms_norm_into()\, so existing
callers are unaffected.

## Triage: A (MERGE NOW)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Swap markdown2 for mistune: fixes [[link]] bug, native \ breaks, Pygments highlighting

- Fixes markdown2 bug where multiple [[text](url)] on same line
  only converted the last one (PDF/DOI/ARX links now all work)

  TOC collection during rendering (no HTML re-parsing needed)

* Fix blog page: collapse blank lines inside HTML blocks before mistune rendering

Liquid templates produce blank lines from {% for %}/{% unless %} tags.
Combined with indentation inside <ul>/<div>/etc, mistune misparsed the
content as indented code blocks.  _collapse_html_blanks() removes blank
lines while inside block-level HTML elements before passing to mistune.

* Fix smart-dashes breaking HTML comments by skipping <!-- ... --> blocks

The _smart_dashes_html regex was converting -- inside HTML comments
to en-dashes, turning --> into –> and breaking comment closing tags.
This caused everything after the broken comment to be swallowed
(hiding footnotes and footer on 11 pages including compiler-effects).

Add <!--.*?--> to the _HTML_SKIP_RE pattern so comments are preserved.

parsing instead of regex post-processing on rendered HTML.  This
correctly preserves dashes inside code spans (e.g. `--zjit`, `--flag`)
which were previously mangled to en-dashes.

Raw HTML blocks still get smart-dash conversion via block_html() in
the renderer, since they bypass inline parsing.

commit.message:
fix: exponential backoff between BT reconnect attempts

Without backoff, a stuck device (e.g. powered off) fires bluetoothctl
connect every ~21s indefinitely, causing ALSA underruns on other A2DP
devices sharing the same adapter due to BT radio contention.

_reconnect_delay(attempt):
  - attempts 1-3 : check_interval (10s, unchanged)
  - attempt 4    : 20s
  - attempt 5    : 40s
  - attempt 6    : 80s
  - attempt 7+   : 160s → capped at 300s (5 min)

Applied to both _monitor_dbus (explicit sleep) and _monitor_polling
(advances last_check to defer the next poll cycle).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove never-hit e2e caches (~2.5 min/run saved) (#390)

* Initial plan

* Remove unused e2e caches that are never hit (~2.5 min/run saved)

Investigation of recent CI runs confirmed all three caches (Docker
images, node_modules, Playwright browsers) always showed "Cache not
found" — because e2e only runs on PRs (never main), so branch-scoped
GitHub Actions caches are never populated for PR branches to inherit.

Removes:
- 3 actions/cache steps (Docker images, node_modules, Playwright)
- Explicit "Save Docker images for caching" step
- Conditional if guards on Docker Buildx/login/build steps
- Cache-hit conditional logic in the install step

Keeps:
- Docker BuildKit layer caching (type=gha) which works cross-branch

commit.message:
Tear down and recreate connection on solution close/open

Previously, closing a solution only cleared the workspace folders in the
lock file while the MCP server process and pipe stayed alive. This meant
copilot-cli stayed connected and received events for an unrelated
workspace when the user opened a different solution.

Now, OnSolutionAfterClosing calls StopConnection() which:
- Cancels the connection CTS (terminates the RPC server loop)
- Disposes the RPC pipe and JsonRpc instance
- Kills the MCP server process
- Removes the lock file from ~/.copilot/ide/

OnSolutionOpened calls StartConnectionAsync() which spins up a fresh
set of pipes, a new MCP server process, and a new lock file. This
matches VS Code's behavior where closing a folder closes the connection
and reopening creates a new one.

Also removes the now-unused UpdateWorkspaceFoldersAsync from
IdeDiscovery, adds try-catch to StartRpcServerAsync for clean
cancellation handling, and adds a LogError helper.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
